description_long: This house features a single-story, rectangular layout with a prominent hip roof in dark gray, giving it a solid and traditional appearance. The facade is symmetrical, with a central entryway accessed by a short set of steps and flanked by evenly spaced windows. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely stucco or painted concrete, and are accented by subtle vertical columns and a horizontal band near the base. The rear side of the house has a recessed section with multiple windows, suggesting a living or communal area with ample natural light. The roof extends uniformly over the structure, providing a cohesive and protective cover. The overall design is simple yet functional, suitable for suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a hip roof, symmetrical facade, central entryway, and evenly spaced windows. The exterior is light-colored with subtle architectural accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central entryway with steps, flanked by two windows on each side and vertical column accents.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, uninterrupted hip roof and the symmetrical arrangement of windows along the facade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the features of view 0 with the entryway and window arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a recessed section with multiple windows, indicating a communal or living area with increased natural light.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with a small set of steps, flanked by columns and no porch or overhang
